ddls:
CREATE TABLE "machines" (
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each machine */,
  "machine_name" TEXT /* Name of the machine */,
  "machine_type" TEXT /* Type of machine (e.g., pump, motor, conveyor) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the machine in the factory */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the machine */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the machine */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the machine was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the machine (e.g., operational, maintenance, offline) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of sensor (e.g., pulsometer, thermometer, pressure sensor) */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine the sensor is installed on */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was installed */,
  "last_calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the last calibration */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the sensor (e.g., operational, faulty, offline) */,
  "calibration_frequency" INTEGER /* Frequency of sensor calibration in days */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensors_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "readings" (
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reading */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that took the reading */,
  "time_stamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the reading */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the reading */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the reading */,
  "sensor_status" TEXT /* Status of the sensor at the time of the reading */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_readings_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "machine_efficiency" (
  "efficiency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each efficiency record */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine the efficiency record belongs to */,
  "time_stamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the efficiency record */,
  "efficiency" REAL /* Efficiency value calculated from sensor readings */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the efficiency */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the machine at the time of the efficiency calculation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("efficiency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_machine_efficiency_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "anomalies" (
  "anomaly_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each anomaly */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine the anomaly belongs to */,
  "time_stamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the anomaly */,
  "anomaly_type" TEXT /* Type of anomaly (e.g., high temperature, low pressure) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the anomaly */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the anomaly (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the anomaly (e.g., active, resolved) */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes related to the resolution of the anomaly */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("anomaly_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_anomalies_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine the maintenance record belongs to */,
  "time_stamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the maintenance */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., routine, repair) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the maintenance (e.g., scheduled, completed) */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Next scheduled maintenance date */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the maintenance */,
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* ID of the technician who performed the maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_technician_id FOREIGN KEY ("technician_id") REFERENCES operators ("operator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operators" (
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operator */,
  "operator_name" TEXT /* Name of the operator */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the operator (e.g., administrator, manager, engineer) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the operator */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the operator */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time of the operator */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the operator's account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator who accessed the system */,
  "time_stamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the operator (e.g., login, view data, edit settings) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES operators ("operator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting (e.g., temperature threshold, pressure limit) */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the setting */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the setting is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)
